heroHeadline: Lebanon–Israel Hold First Direct Talks in 3 Decades; US Seizes Iranian Tanker as Ceasefire Standoff Deepens
heroSubtitle: Lebanese and Israeli ambassadors met in Washington on Apr 23 — the first direct Lebanon-Israel diplomatic contact in approximately three decades — seeking a one-month extension of the 10-day ceasefire (expiring Apr 26) with Rubio facilitating. Simultaneously, the US seized an Iranian oil tanker in the Indian Ocean and Iran threatened to keep the Strait of Hormuz closed, deepening the coercive standoff with US-Iran talks frozen and JD Vance's Pakistan trip called off. Lebanon President Aoun called it 'an opportunity that may not arise again.' 11 ceasefires nominally in force globally.
footerNote: Data sourced from UN, African Union, IGAD, Crisis Group, USIP, PA-X Database, and Tier 1–3 sources across all media poles.
